report:
ClearWave Analytics – Research Memo
Date: June 12, 2026

I. Company Overview and Business Model

– ClearWave Analytics, founded in 2017 and headquartered in Denver, CO, is a private cloud-based data analytics platform specializing in real-time environmental and industrial IoT sensor data processing.
– The company offers SaaS subscriptions for its core analytics platform and customizable AI-driven predictive maintenance and anomaly detection modules.
– Key revenue streams include:
  • Platform licenses (SaaS subscription tiers ranging from $5K to $75K annual per client)
  • Customized analytics modules and AI model integration (project-based fees)
  • Data API access for third-party developers
– ClearWave's platform ingests data from over 2.5 million active IoT sensors globally, serving clients mainly in utilities, manufacturing, oil & gas, and smart city infrastructure.
– The latest disclosed financial figures (FY 2025):
  • Revenue: $96.5M (up 48% YoY from $65.2M in 2024)
  • Gross Margin: 76%
  • EBITDA Margin: 22%
  • Net Income: $12.4M (13% margin)
  • Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR): $88.7M
  • Customer count: 215 enterprise clients, including 35 Fortune 500 companies
  • Customer retention rate: 93%

II. Market and Industry Context

– The global industrial IoT analytics market is estimated at $7.2B in 2025 with projected CAGR of 18% through 2030 driven by increasing sensor deployment, demand for operational efficiency, and AI adoption.
– Increasing regulatory focus on emissions and safety coupled with digital transformation initiatives creates strong demand tailwinds.
– Major competitors include:
  • SensorLogic (privately held, mid-market focus, ~120 clients)
  • NexuSense (subsidiary of large conglomerate, broad IoT platform offering)
  • EdgeMetrics (smaller player focused on hardware plus software bundles)

III. Competitive Advantages

– Real-time AI analytics with proprietary signal-denoising and anomaly detection algorithms offering 15-25% better predictive accuracy over competitors (based on client case studies).
– Highly modular SaaS architecture enabling quick deployment and customization.
– ClearWave’s cloud-native platform scales horizontally with near-zero latency, optimized for industrial edge use cases.
– Strong customer relationships with multi-year contracts and 8-12 month average sales cycles indicative of high switching costs.
– Robust R&D team of ~60 engineers focused exclusively on AI and sensor-data optimization.

IV. Customer and Sales Overview

– 215 enterprise clients across 4 main sectors: utilities (40%), manufacturing (30%), oil & gas (15%), smart cities/infrastructure (15%).
– Customer concentration: Top 10 clients represent 42% of revenue, but long-term contracts and high retention mitigate risk.
– Sales primarily through direct enterprise sales with an 18% YoY increase in new customer acquisition in 2025.
– Average contract value (ACV): $413K, growing steadily with upselling of AI modules.

V. Financial Performance

– Revenue growth: 48% YoY in 2025, driven by expansion in AI modules adoption and increased IoT sensor data volumes.
– Gross margin steady at 76%, driven by efficient cloud infrastructure and software licensing.
– EBITDA of $21.2M (22% margin) indicating operational scalability.
– Positive net income achieved in 2025 after 3 years of profitable growth trajectory.
– Cash flow positive with $18M free cash flow generated in 2025.
– Balance sheet strong, $12M cash and no long-term debt.

VI. Growth Opportunities

– Market expansion into Asia-Pacific where IoT sensor deployments for utilities and manufacturing are accelerating, representing ~30% addressable market growth.
– Development of standardized predictive modules for additional verticals such as agriculture and transportation.
– Potential add-on acquisitions of IoT device telemetry startups to vertically integrate data ingestion.
– Strategic partnerships with leading cloud providers for co-selling and deeper technical integration.
– Increasing ARPU through advanced AI features and enterprise tier upsells.

VII. Risks and Challenges

– Competitive pressure from large cloud giants developing in-house IoT analytics platforms.
– Customer concentration and relatively long sales cycles may impact near-term revenue visibility.
– Dependence on continued innovation to maintain predictive accuracy advantage.
– Potential cybersecurity risks given sensitivity of client operational data.

VIII. Recent Developments

– Closed a $15M Series C in Q1 2026 led by GreenRiver Capital to fund international expansion and R&D.
– Recently signed a multi-year contract with Pacific Northwest Utilities, projected $8M revenue over 5 years.
– Launched ClearWatch AI suite in late 2025, leading to 25% module adoption among existing clients within 6 months.

IX. Summary of Key Points

– ClearWave is a high-growth SaaS player in a niche but rapidly growing industrial IoT analytics market.
– Strong technology differentiation with proprietary AI and real-time data abilities.
– Healthy financial profile with high margins, positive net income and cash flow.
– Compelling growth runway through geographic expansion, product line extension, and potential M&A.
– Balanced risks primarily around competitive dynamics and execution on growth plans.
reasoning:
Okay, looking at ClearWave Analytics, this is a company in the industrial IoT analytics space, which is big and growing fast given the push for digital transformation in industries like utilities and manufacturing. They have a solid niche with real-time AI analytics, a proprietary technology edge improving predictive maintenance accuracy.

Let me analyze the financials here. Their revenue grew 48% year-over-year in 2025 to about $96.5M, which is impressive growth at this scale. The gross margin at 76% is healthy for a SaaS/data platform, reflecting relatively low cost of goods sold and efficient cloud infrastructure. EBITDA margin at 22% with positive net income indicates excellent operating leverage. They’re cash flow positive and balance sheet is clean—no debt and meaningful cash—so financial health is very strong.

Competition includes a few players but no global cloud monster dominant in industrial IoT analytics; ClearWave seems to have a defendable position via proprietary AI and long-term contracts. Customer retention is 93%, which is terrific for SaaS, giving revenue stability. Some customer concentration exists but mitigated by multi-year contracts.

Growth opportunities look robust, with geographic expansion into Asia-Pacific and new verticals as big levers. They also have organic upsell via AI modules, increasing their average contract values. Plus, their recent Series C funding suggests investor confidence and resources to scale.

Risks include large cloud providers eventually moving into this space, possibly squeezing margins or taking market share. Also, cybersecurity risks and the need to keep innovating AI capabilities. Sales cycles are long, typical for enterprise, so they must maintain pipeline discipline.

Overall, ClearWave seems to be a high-quality, high-growth SaaS/AI company in a strategic market with strong financials and competitive moat. The opportunity for continued market share gains and expansion is compelling, and their profitability profile means growth is likely sustainable.

I would rate the investment decision as a strong_yes based on their defensible tech, strong FCF generation, client base, and growth prospects, balanced against manageable risks like competition and execution.

verdict.evidence_pro:
1. ClearWave holds a proprietary AI-powered real-time analytics platform with measurable 15-25% predictive accuracy advantage.
2. Strong financial performance with 48% YoY revenue growth to $96.5M, 76% gross margin, positive net income and EBITDA margins over 20%.
3. High customer retention (93%) and impressive enterprise client roster including Fortune 500 companies.
4. Clear growth runway via geographic expansion into Asia-Pacific and new verticals like agriculture and transportation.
5. Large market opportunity in rapidly growing global industrial IoT analytics (18% CAGR).
6. Recent successful Series C funding and signed multi-year contracts enhance scalability and growth visibility.
verdict.evidence_contra:
1. Competitive threat from large cloud providers potentially entering industrial IoT analytics space.
2. Customer concentration risk with top 10 clients representing 42% of revenue.
3. Long sales cycles (8-12 months) could impact short-term revenue predictability.
4. Cybersecurity risks related to handling sensitive operational data.
5. Dependence on continued AI innovation to sustain competitive advantage.
6. Execution risk associated with international expansion and product line extension.
verdict.similar_deals:
1. SilverPeak Ventures’ $120M growth equity investment in TraceSense Analytics, a SaaS industrial IoT analytics platform, in 2024.
2. Granite Capital’s $85M buyout of EdgeIQ in 2023, focusing on AI-driven manufacturing sensor data analysis.
3. BlackHawk Partners’ $60M growth round in SmartGrid Insights, an energy sector IoT analytics SaaS, in early 2025.
4. Maplewood Growth’s $50M investment in TerraSense AI, specializing in agricultural predictive analytics using IoT data, in 2025.
verdict.key_information_requested:
1. Detailed client contract terms and churn analysis beyond headline retention rates.
2. Roadmap and pipeline for AI algorithm improvements and product module launches.
3. Sales funnel data to assess pace and drivers of new customer acquisition.
4. Competitive intelligence on current and future moves by cloud giants in industrial IoT space.
5. Cybersecurity measures and audits to assess data protection risks.
6. Detailed international growth strategy and local regulatory compliance in Asia-Pacific markets.
